en.name: A+ Academics Foundation / A+ APTC
en.youth_short: Educational support, enrichment activities and youth development on St. Eustatius.
en.youth_where_can_you_go: Homework support, tutoring, computer access, career guidance, sports, arts, volunteering and personal development.
en.youth_how_it_works: Young people join voluntarily and receive guidance from youth leaders, mentors and educators.
en.professional_summary: A+ Academics Foundation supports the educational, personal and social development of young people on St. Eustatius.
en.professional_referral_or_access: Young people can enroll in the programmes. Activities are offered throughout the school year.
en.age_range: Children and young people in primary and secondary education

who_we_are.en: A+ Academics Foundation supports the educational, personal and social development of young people on St. Eustatius. We provide educational support, enrichment activities and opportunities to prepare for further education and work.

who_for.en: Children and young people in primary and secondary school, students who need academic guidance, young people preparing for further education or work, families and teachers.

what_help.en: Homework support and tutoring, computer-based learning, youth development, career guidance, sports, music and arts, volunteering, cultural education and family education.

how_to_access.en: Young people can enroll voluntarily in a programme. Their needs and interests are discussed so they can join suitable activities.

how_we_help.en: We use mentoring, coaching, tutoring, learning by doing, skills workshops, community engagement and career exploration in a safe and supportive environment.

duration.en: The duration depends on the programme. Some students participate during the school year, while others remain involved for several years.

partners.en: We work with young people, parents, teachers, youth leaders, mentors and community partners.

contact.en: De Graafweg #13, Oranjestad, St. Eustatius. Phone: +599 318 0788. Email: info@aptcyouth.org. Website: aptcyouth.org.

short_description.en: A+ Academics Foundation supports educational, personal and social development. Its mission is to build confidence, encourage school retention and create academic and personal growth opportunities.

target_group.en: Children and adolescents in primary and secondary education, young people needing academic support, students preparing for education or employment, parents and teachers.

support_offer.en: Educational, recreational and developmental activities in a safe environment, with guidance from youth leaders, mentors and educators.

services.en: Homework assistance, computer laboratory access, youth development, career exploration, sports, music and arts, volunteering, cultural education, family education and teacher mentoring.

methods.en: Positive youth development, mentoring and coaching, experiential learning, tutoring, skills workshops, community engagement, career exploration and family involvement.

execution.en: Structured year-round activities include after-school support, workshops, sports, arts, entrepreneurship, community service and cultural projects.

problems.en: Academic difficulties, low confidence or motivation, limited educational resources, career planning, personal and social development and family involvement in education.

trajectory.en: Enrollment, assessment of needs and interests, participation in relevant activities, ongoing mentoring, progress monitoring and transition to another education or career pathway.

average_duration.en: Varies by need and programme; often throughout a school year and sometimes for several years.

when_appropriate.en: When a young person needs academic support, enrichment, career orientation or personal development opportunities.

criteria.en: Programmes are generally voluntary. Contact A+ APTC to discuss enrollment and current availability.

indications_required.en: No formal indication is described in the supplied information.

contact_details.en: De Graafweg #13, Oranjestad. Phone: +599 318 0788. Email: info@aptcyouth.org. Website: aptcyouth.org.

partners.en: Parents, teachers, youth leaders, mentors and community partners.

other_information.en: Programmes are built around academics and enrichment, business and entrepreneurship, and social, cultural and community awareness.

en.name: Zorg en Jeugd Caribisch Nederland (ZJCN) – Youth Care Department
en.youth_short: Support for children, young people and families who need extra help or guidance.
en.youth_where_can_you_go: Outpatient youth care, parenting and family support, foster care and supported living.
en.youth_how_it_works: You can be referred through a doctor, school or ECE, or visit during walk-in hours.
en.professional_summary: Youth care for parenting difficulties, developmental or behavioural problems, abuse, family problems and supported living.
en.professional_referral_or_access: Referral is possible through a GP, school or partner organisation. Walk-in consultation is also available.
en.professional_notes: ZJCN operates under the Dutch Ministry of Health, Welfare and Sport.

who_we_are.en: We are the Youth Care Department of ZJCN. We support children, young people and families on the BES islands. Our mission is: Every child a safe place.

who_for.en: Children and young people having difficulties at home, at school or with themselves; children facing violence or abuse; parents and families needing support; and young people who cannot live at home temporarily.

what_help.en: Support at home, parenting support, family support, foster care, supported living and help with difficulties at home, school or daily life.

how_to_access.en: Through a general practitioner, school or ECE, or by visiting during walk-in hours. Together we discuss which help fits best.

how_we_help.en: We start with an introduction, listen to what is happening, make a plan, set goals, provide guidance and regularly evaluate and adjust the support.

duration.en: The duration differs for each person. Some need short-term help and others need longer support. The situation is evaluated regularly.

partners.en: General practitioners, schools, ECE, the Guardianship Council, the Public Entity of Statia and MHC.

contact.en: Mazinga building, Oranjestad. Phone: +599 318 3379. Email: info@jeugdzorgcn.com. Website: zorgenjeugdcn.nl.

organisation_name.en: Zorg en Jeugd Caribisch Nederland (ZJCN) – Youth Care Department

short_description.en: ZJCN works to improve the position and well-being of children. Its mission is: Every child a safe place.

target_group.en: Children and young people with parenting, developmental, behavioural or family-related problems, including child abuse, and their families.

support_offer.en: Outpatient youth care, foster care, supported living, parenting support, family support, case management and coordination.

services.en: Outpatient guidance, family support, parenting support, foster care and supported living arrangements.

methods.en: Structured guidance, CFA methodology in Youth and Parenting Care+, FFP and basic communication principles.

execution.en: Support is tailored to the young person and family. Supported living includes structured and continuous guidance.

problems.en: Child abuse, domestic violence, parenting difficulties, developmental and behavioural problems, out-of-home living and family problems.

trajectory.en: Registration, intake, briefing, assignment of a caseworker and phased support (phases 1, 2 and 3).

average_duration.en: Depends on needs and progress. Foster care may continue until age 18, with regular evaluation.

when_appropriate.en: For parenting difficulties, youth problems or when a young person needs guided living outside the home.

criteria.en: Referral is possible through a GP, school or partner organisation. People can also attend walk-in consultation hours.

indications_required.en: Depends on the service and route of access.

contact_details.en: Mazinga building, Oranjestad. Phone: +599 318 3379. Email: info@jeugdzorgcn.com. Website: zorgenjeugdcn.nl.

opening_hours.en: Monday, Wednesday and Friday 08:00–12:00. Tuesday and Thursday 13:00–16:30.

partners.en: General practitioners, Public Entity of Statia, Jeugdbescherming West, KPCN, MHC, Probation Service, ECE and the Guardianship Council.

other_information.en: ZJCN operates under the Ministry of Health, Welfare and Sport (VWS) of the Netherlands.

address.en: Catherine Lespier Road 1, Bay Brow, St. Eustatius

en.name: Mental Health Caribbean (MHC)
en.youth_short: Mental health support for children, young people and their families.
en.youth_where_can_you_go: Help with anxiety, sadness, anger, concentration, sleep, eating, trauma, relationships or addiction.
en.youth_how_it_works: A referral from a GP or pediatrician is required. MHC then schedules an intake.
en.professional_summary: Outpatient assessment, diagnosis, treatment and family support for children and adolescents with psychological or psychiatric difficulties.
en.professional_referral_or_access: Referral by a GP or pediatrician is required.
en.type: Child and youth mental health care

who_we_are.en: Mental Health Caribbean helps children, young people and families when they are struggling with feelings, thoughts or behaviour. We believe every child deserves to grow up safe, healthy and confident.

who_for.en: Children and young people aged 0 to 18, and parents or caregivers who want support for their child.

what_help.en: Worry or anxiety, sadness, anger, concentration problems, sleep or eating difficulties, problems at home or school, trauma, and gaming, alcohol or drug problems.

how_to_access.en: You need a referral from a family doctor or pediatrician. We then schedule a first meeting.

how_we_help.en: We look at the child and the people around them. Support may include conversations with a psychologist, therapy, group programmes or family support.

duration.en: The process includes getting acquainted, assessment, treatment and completion. Duration depends on needs and goals.

partners.en: We involve parents, caregivers, schools and partner organisations when appropriate.

contact.en: Catherine Lespier Road 1, Bay Brow, St. Eustatius. Phone: +599 318 0099 / +599 319 5677. Email: secretariaat@mentalhealthcaribbean.com.

short_description.en: MHC aims to give children and adolescents a fair chance to develop into resilient adults through outpatient mental health assessment, treatment and support.

target_group.en: Children and young people aged 0 to 18 with psychological or psychiatric complaints, and their families.

support_offer.en: Assessment, diagnosis, individual and group treatment, psychiatric care and family support.

services.en: Mental health assessment, psychological treatment, individual and group therapy, family treatment, psychiatric consultation and guidance.

methods.en: A systemic approach involving the family and the child's environment. Relevant life areas such as school, housing, social functioning and daily activities are considered.

execution.en: The family is involved in assessment, guidance and treatment. Individual or group treatment is offered according to need.

problems.en: Mental health concerns, psychiatric disorders, emotional or behavioural difficulties, trauma, attachment issues, family difficulties and addiction.

trajectory.en: Intake, assessment and treatment. Goals are set with the child and family, followed by completion and an aftercare plan.

average_duration.en: Depends on assessment, treatment goals and the needs of the child and family.

when_appropriate.en: When a child or young person experiences psychological or psychiatric difficulties.

criteria.en: A referral letter from a GP or pediatrician is required and is processed by the intake service.

indications_required.en: Yes, referral by a GP or pediatrician is required.

contact_details.en: Catherine Lespier Road 1, Bay Brow. Phone: +599 318 0099 / +599 319 5677. Email: secretariaat@mentalhealthcaribbean.com. Website: mentalhealthcaribbean.com.

partners.en: Parents, caregivers, schools and relevant care partners.

other_information.en: Treatment considers all relevant areas of the child's life and may combine child therapy with family or systemic support.

address.en: Cottageroad z/n, Oranjestad, St. Eustatius, Caribbean Netherlands

en.name: Guardianship Council Caribbean Netherlands / Voogdijraad Caribisch Nederland
en.youth_short: Investigation, advice and statutory support for children, young people and parents.
en.youth_where_can_you_go: Child protection concerns, HALT, child maintenance, international child matters and representation of a child's interests.
en.youth_how_it_works: Some services can be contacted directly. HALT requires referral by police or the Public Prosecutor.
en.professional_summary: The Guardianship Council conducts investigations, advises courts and services, and carries out statutory youth and family law tasks.
en.professional_referral_or_access: Direct contact is possible for case advice and child maintenance. HALT requires a police or Public Prosecutor referral.
en.type: Child protection, youth justice and family law

who_we_are.en: The Guardianship Council is there for children, young people and parents. We listen, investigate and advise so children can grow up safely, healthily and happily.

who_for.en: Children and young people aged 0 to 18, families with safety or development concerns, young people in contact with police or truancy services, and parents with child maintenance questions.

what_help.en: Case investigation, HALT for minor offences, advice about child maintenance, international child cases and representation through a special guardian.

how_to_access.en: Concerns can be reported by parents, schools or professionals. Child maintenance advice is directly accessible. HALT requires referral by police or the Public Prosecutor.

how_we_help.en: Through conversations, investigations, advice, solution-finding and referrals. HALT uses educational, restorative and work assignments to help young people learn from an offence.

duration.en: Duration depends on the service. A HALT programme is usually completed within approximately 100 days.

partners.en: Police, Public Prosecutor, truancy officers, schools, ZJCN, MHC and Probation Service Caribbean Netherlands.

contact.en: Cottageroad z/n, Oranjestad, St. Eustatius. Email: voogdijraadbes@rijksdienstcn.com. Office hours: Tuesday and Thursday 09:00–11:00. HALT: haltcn@rijksdienstcn.com.

organisation_name.en: Guardianship Council Caribbean Netherlands (Voogdijraad Caribisch Nederland)

short_description.en: The Council performs child protection investigations, juvenile justice advice, HALT, child maintenance, Central Authority and special guardian tasks.

target_group.en: Children and young people aged 0 to 18, young people in contact with justice services, and families where there are safety, development or upbringing concerns.

support_offer.en: Investigations, statutory advice and youth and family law tasks. The Council is not a treatment organisation.

services.en: Child protection investigation, juvenile justice advice, HALT, child maintenance advice, Central Authority work and special guardianship.

methods.en: HALT methodology, tailored educational assignments, restorative activities, responsibility and behavioural change.

execution.en: HALT includes an intake with the young person and parents, an intervention plan, educational or work assignments, follow-up and completion.

problems.en: Child safety concerns, minor criminal behaviour, truancy, boundary-crossing behaviour, child maintenance and international child matters.

trajectory.en: Referral, intake, tailored programme, assignments, monitoring, completion and evaluation.

average_duration.en: HALT usually lasts up to 100 days.

when_appropriate.en: For child safety investigation, child maintenance advice or a HALT response to a minor offence.

criteria.en: No referral is needed for case advice or child maintenance. HALT is for ages 12–18 and requires referral by police, truancy officer or Public Prosecutor.

indications_required.en: HALT: yes. Other advice services: no formal referral.

contact_details.en: Cottageroad z/n, Oranjestad. Email: voogdijraadbes@rijksdienstcn.com. Website: rijksdienstcn.com/voogdijraad. HALT: haltcn@rijksdienstcn.com / haltcn.com.

partners.en: Police, Public Prosecutor, truancy officers, schools, ZJCN, MHC and Probation Service Caribbean Netherlands.

other_information.en: Successful completion of HALT can prevent a criminal record.

address.en: Fort Street 13, Windwardside, Saba, Caribbean Netherlands

en.name: Saba Lions Foundation
en.youth_short: Community projects, learning opportunities, health activities and support for children and families.
en.youth_where_can_you_go: Educational workshops, youth programmes, food support, health promotion, culture, recreation and volunteering.
en.youth_how_it_works: Most activities are open to the public and do not require a formal referral.
en.professional_summary: A charitable foundation supporting humanitarian, educational, health, youth development and community service initiatives on Saba.
en.professional_referral_or_access: Most activities are freely accessible. People may self-register or be referred by a school, organisation or government agency.
en.professional_notes: Services vary according to community needs and available resources.
en.age_range: Children, young people, families and the wider Saba community

who_we_are.en: The Saba Lions Foundation is a non-profit organisation that supports projects making life better for people on Saba. We work with volunteers and partners to build a caring and strong community.

who_for.en: Children and young people, parents and families, older people, people needing extra support, schools and community groups.

what_help.en: Learning workshops, youth programmes, health and wellness projects, food support, scholarships when available, cultural and recreational activities and volunteering.

how_to_access.en: Join through a school or community organisation, sign up for an activity or respond to community outreach. Most activities do not require referral.

how_we_help.en: We organise group activities and workshops, build skills, support families and youth, encourage volunteering and connect people with specialist services when needed.

duration.en: Some activities last a few hours or one day, projects may last several weeks, and some programmes recur each year.

partners.en: Schools, community organisations, government services, the Saba Leo Club and local partners.

contact.en: Fort Street 13, Windwardside, Saba. Phone: +599 416 3700. Email: sabalc.sectreas@gmail.com. Facebook: Saba Lions Club.

short_description.en: A non-profit charitable organisation supporting humanitarian, educational, health, youth development and community service initiatives.

target_group.en: Children and youth, families, senior citizens, vulnerable households, community organisations, schools and people facing social or financial challenges.

support_offer.en: Community projects, educational initiatives, volunteer programmes, health activities, family activities and partnerships with local organisations.

services.en: Workshops, youth development, family strengthening, outreach, food assistance, health awareness, educational support, culture, recreation and volunteering.

methods.en: Community-based and strengths-focused work, volunteer engagement, partnerships, prevention, early intervention, capacity building, experiential learning and service learning.

execution.en: Needs are identified with the community, partners and volunteers are engaged, and group activities are delivered and evaluated.

problems.en: Social inclusion, youth engagement, family well-being, educational development, food insecurity, health promotion and civic responsibility.

trajectory.en: Engagement starts through outreach, a partner referral, a request or participation in a specific project or activity.

average_duration.en: From half-day activities to projects of several weeks, recurring annual programmes or ongoing volunteering.

when_appropriate.en: For community support, educational activities, volunteer opportunities, family programmes, youth projects or outreach.

criteria.en: Most activities are freely accessible. Self-registration and informal referrals are possible.

contact_details.en: Fort Street 13, Windwardside. Phone: +599 416 3700. Email: sabalc.sectreas@gmail.com. Facebook: Saba Lions Club.

partners.en: Schools, families, government entities, community organisations, youth organisations and the Saba Leo Club.

other_information.en: The Foundation does not provide clinical, therapeutic or crisis services, but may refer to specialist agencies.

address.en: Clement Sorton Street, main building of Saba Roads, The Bottom, Saba

address.en: 11 Samuel Charles Street, The Bottom (Old Bottom Bean), Saba

en.name: Child Focus Foundation
en.youth_short: Creative and developmental after-school activities in a safe, inclusive environment.
en.youth_where_can_you_go: Music, dance, theatre, arts, leadership, life skills, culture, heritage and mentorship.
en.youth_how_it_works: Children aged 4–12 can usually join without referral. A parent, caregiver or social worker may also make contact.
en.professional_summary: Child Focus nurtures the social, emotional and recreational development of children through creative, age-appropriate programmes.
en.professional_referral_or_access: Services are accessible to all children aged 4–12, including visiting children. No referral is normally needed.

who_we_are.en: We are the Child Focus Foundation. We help children on Saba grow, learn and have fun in a safe and caring place.

who_for.en: Children aged 4 to 12. All children are welcome, including visiting children.

what_help.en: Choir and steel pan, dance, theatre, arts and crafts, leadership and life skills, cultural activities, mentorship and a safe supervised place after school.

how_to_access.en: Most children can simply join. A parent, caregiver or social worker may contact us if a child could benefit from extra support or structured activities.

how_we_help.en: Children learn by doing through games, art, music and teamwork. We help them build confidence, friendships and a sense of inclusion and safety.

duration.en: There is no fixed time. Children can join as long as the programme fits their interests and support needs.

partners.en: Parents, caregivers, schools, social workers, community organisations and other youth partners.

contact.en: JQH9+78F, Windwardside, Saba. Phone: +599 416 2318. Email: childfocussaba@gmail.com. Website: childfocussaba.org.

short_description.en: The Foundation nurtures children's social, emotional and recreational development through creative, age-appropriate activities in a supportive and inclusive environment.

support_offer.en: Performing and creative arts, leadership and lifestyle development, culture and heritage, mentorship and supervised after-school activities.

services.en: Steel pan, dance, theatre, choir, visual arts, leadership, life skills, cultural activities and mentorship.

methods.en: Social-emotional learning, arts-based learning, project-based learning, inclusion, creativity and community engagement.

execution.en: Practical hands-on daily and weekly activities, including music, dance, theatre, choir and visual arts workshops.

problems.en: The need for a safe supervised after-school environment and opportunities for social, emotional, creative and recreational development.

trajectory.en: Information is gathered about the child and their interests, suitable programmes are suggested, and participation is followed through regular contact with activity leaders and parents.

average_duration.en: Depends on the programme and the child's interests and support needs.

when_appropriate.en: For any child aged 4–12 who would benefit from a safe, creative and structured after-school programme.

criteria.en: No referral is required. Parents can register directly; a social worker may also contact the Foundation.

contact_details.en: JQH9+78F, Windwardside. Phone: +599 416 2318. Email: childfocussaba@gmail.com. Website: childfocussaba.org.

partners.en: Parents, schools, social workers, community organisations and other partners supporting children and youth.

other_information.en: The organisation aims to provide each Saban child with access to a positive after-school programme.

en.name: Guana Chat 918
en.youth_short: Free and anonymous support for children and young people. Call 918 when you need someone to talk to.
en.youth_where_can_you_go: Call about anything that worries you or when you need immediate emotional support.
en.youth_how_it_works: Call 918 free of charge and anonymously.
en.professional_summary: Guana Chat 918 is a free and anonymous helpline for children and young people in the Caribbean Netherlands.
en.professional_referral_or_access: No referral is needed. Young people can call 918 directly.

who_we_are.en: Guana Chat 918 is a free and anonymous helpline for children and young people.

who_for.en: For children and young people who are worried, struggling or need someone to talk to.

what_help.en: You can call when you feel sad, afraid, angry or unsure, when something is happening at home or at school, or when you simply need to talk to someone.

how_to_access.en: Call 918 free of charge and anonymously.

how_we_help.en: You speak with someone who listens and thinks along with you.

duration.en: A conversation lasts as long as needed within the contact moment.

short_description.en: Guana Chat 918 is a free and anonymous helpline for children and young people in the Caribbean Netherlands.

support_offer.en: Free and anonymous phone support for children and young people who need someone to talk to.

services.en: Listening, emotional support and guidance towards further help.

problems.en: Any concern or situation a young person wants to discuss.

average_duration.en: One or more calls as needed.

when_appropriate.en: When a child or young person needs a low-threshold, anonymous conversation.

indications_required.en: No. Young people can call directly.

opening_hours.en: Check the website for current availability.

other_information.en: The service is free and anonymous.

en.name: Caribbean Netherlands Police Force (KPCN)
en.youth_short: Call 911 when there is immediate danger or you urgently need police assistance.
en.youth_where_can_you_go: Immediate danger, violence, abuse, threats or another police emergency.
en.professional_summary: KPCN provides police assistance and emergency response in the Caribbean Netherlands.

who_we_are.en: The Caribbean Netherlands Police Force (KPCN) is the police service for Bonaire, Saba and St. Eustatius.

who_for.en: For anyone who sees immediate danger, feels unsafe or urgently needs police assistance.

what_help.en: Call 911 in case of immediate danger, violence, abuse, threats or another emergency requiring police assistance.

how_to_access.en: Call 911 in an emergency or immediate danger.

how_we_help.en: The police assess the situation and respond when there is an emergency or immediate danger.

contact.en: Call 911 in an emergency or immediate danger.

short_description.en: KPCN is the police service for Bonaire, Saba and St. Eustatius.

target_group.en: Residents, young people, parents, professionals and organisations dealing with safety or emergency situations.

support_offer.en: Police assistance and emergency response in case of immediate danger, violence, threats or other situations requiring urgent police assistance.

services.en: Police response, safety intervention and emergency assistance.

execution.en: Call 911 and provide the location and nature of the emergency.

problems.en: Immediate danger, violence, threats, abuse and crime.

when_appropriate.en: In case of emergency, immediate danger or urgent need for police assistance.

criteria.en: Call 911 in an emergency or immediate danger.

contact_details.en: Call 911 in an emergency or immediate danger.

partners.en: Emergency, justice and care services.

other_information.en: Use 911 only when immediate assistance is needed.
